creator: dwmalone@maths.tcd.ie
text:
> >Synopsis:       calender.judaic is out of date as of 1 Jan 02

Josef - I've made a few small changes to the calendar file which
you sent us. I've named you as the maintainer, removed trailing
whitespace and removed the space between "Adar" and "!". There was
an "I" after "Adar" in the old version of the line "Be happy, It's
Adar!" - I presume you deliberately removed that. If this looks
OK then I'll commit it to -current and ask for it to be added
in time for 4.5-RELEASE.

How do you get the updates for the calendar file? Maybe we should
produce a patch to calendar which can calculate the important days
in the Judaic calendar and then we can write a file which doesn't
need to be replaced every year?

(I know that this isn't feasable for some calendars, which rely on
observation to decide when festivals are. If I remember correctly
the judaic calendar is predictable, but it has a rather long cycle?)

	David.


/*
 * Judaic Calendar. Maintained by Josef Grosch <jgrosch@mooseriver.com>.
 *
 * $FreeBSD$
 *
 */

#ifndef _calendar_judaic_
#define _calendar_judaic_

/*
 * Jewish calendar for the CE year 2002
 * 17 Tevet 5762 - 26 Tevet 5763
 */

01/05*	Parashat Shemoth
01/12*	Parashat Va-Era
01/12*	Shabbat Mevarekhim
01/14*	Rosh Chodesh Shevat (Beginning of the month of Shevat)
01/19*	Parashat Bo
01/26*	Parashat Be-Shallah

01/28*	Tu bi-Shevat
02/02*	Parashat Yitro
02/09*	Parashat Mishpatim
02/09*	Shekalim

02/12*	Rosh Chodesh Adar (Beginning of the month of AdarI)

02/16*	Parashat Terumah
02/23*	Parashat Tezavveh
02/23*	Zaklor
02/25*	Fast of Esther (Battle of Purim; 1 day before Purim)
02/26*	Purim (Feast of Lots; 30 days before Pesach)
02/27*	Shushan Purim
03/02*	Parah

03/09*	Parashat Va-Yakhel

03/27*	Erev Pesah
03/27*	Fast of the First born

03/29*	Pesach (Second Day of Passover)
03/29*	Sefirat ha-Omer begins (Counting of the Omer)
03/30*	Hol ha-Mo'ed
03/30*	Omer 2nd day
03/31*	Hol ha-Mo'ed
03/31*	Omer 3rd day
04/01*	Hol ha-Mo'ed
04/01*	Omer 4th day
04/02*	Hol ha-Mo'ed
04/02*	Omer 5th day
04/03*	Omer 6th day
04/03*	Pesach (Seventh Day of Passover)

04/05*	Omer 8th day
04/06*	Omer 9th day
04/06*	Parashat Shemini
04/06*	Shabbat Mevarekhim
04/07*	Omer 10th day
04/08*	Omer 11th day
04/09*	Omer 12th day
04/09*	Yom ha-Sho'ah (Remembrance of the Holocaust)
04/10*	Omer 13th day
04/11*	Omer 14th day
04/12*	Omer 15th day
04/12*	Rosh Chodesh Iyar (Beginning of the month of Iyar)
04/13*	Omer 16th day
04/13*	Parashat Mezora
04/13*	Parashat Tazria
04/13*	Rosh Chodesh Iyar (Beginning of the month of Iyar)
04/14*	Omer 17th day
04/15*	Omer 18th day
04/16*	Omer 19th day
04/16*	Yom ha-Zikaron (Israeli Memorial Day)
04/17*	Omer 20th day
04/17*	Yom ha-Atzmaut (Israel Independence Day)
04/18*	Omer 21th day
04/19*	Omer 22th day
04/20*	Omer 23th day
04/20*	Parashat Aharei Mot
04/20*	Parashat Kedoshim
04/21*	Omer 24th day
04/22*	Omer 25th day
04/23*	Omer 26th day
04/24*	Omer 27th day
04/25*	Omer 28th day
04/26*	Omer 29th day

04/28*	Omer 31th day

04/30*	Omer 33th day
05/01*	Omer 34th day

05/03*	Omer 36th day
05/04*	Omer 37th day
05/04*	Parashat Be-Har

05/12*	Rosh Chodesh Sivan (Beginning of the month of Sivan)
05/13*	Omer 46th day

05/19*	Omer 42th day

06/01*	Parashat Be-Ha'alotkha
06/08*	Parashat Shelah

06/10*	Rosh Chodesh Tammuz (Beginning of the month of Tammuz)

07/06*	Parashat Mattot
07/06*	Shabbat Mevarekhim
07/10*	Rosh Chodesh Av (Beginning of the month of Av)
07/13*	Parashat Devarim
07/13*	Shabbat Hazon
07/18*	Fast of Tish'a B'Av (Babylon/Rome destroys Holy Temple)
07/20*	Parashat Va-Ethannan
07/20*	Shabbat Nahamu
07/27*	Parashat Ekev
08/03*	Parashat Re'eh
08/03*	Shabbat Mevarekhim
08/08*	Rosh Chodesh Elul (Beginning of the month of Elul)
08/09*	First sounding of the Shofar
08/09*	Rosh Chodesh Elul (Beginning of the month of Elul)
08/10*	Parashat Shofetim
08/17*	Parashat Ki Teze
08/24*	Parashat Ki Tavo
08/31*	Parashat Nizzavim

08/31*	Selihot
09/06*	Erev Rosh ha-Shanah (Jewish Lunar New Year; 5763)

09/07*	Rosh Chodesh Tishri (Beginning of the month of Tishri)
09/08*	Second Day of Rosh ha-Shanah (Jewish Lunar New Year; 5763)
09/09*	Fast of Gedalya (Murder of Gedalya and subsequent Exile; 1 day after Rosh Hashanah)
09/14*	Parashat Ha'azinu
09/14*	Shabbat Shuvah
09/15*	Erev Yom Kippur (Kol Nidra)

09/20*	Erev Sucot

09/22*	Sucot
09/23*	Hol ha-Mo'ed
09/24*	Hol ha-Mo'ed
09/25*	Hol ha-Mo'ed
09/26*	Hol ha-Mo'ed
09/27*	Hoshana Rabba (7th day of Sucot)
09/28*	Shmini Atzeret (8th Day of Gathering; 1 day after Sucot)
09/28*	Yizkor
09/29*	Simchas Torah (Rejoicing of the Law)
10/05*	Parashat Bereshit
10/05*	Shabbat Mevarekhim
10/06*	Rosh Chodesh Heshvan (Beginning of the month of Heshvan)
10/07*	Rosh Chodesh Heshvan (Beginning of the month of Heshvan)
10/12*	Parashat No'ah
10/19*	Parashat Lekh Lekha
10/26*	Parashat Va-Yera
11/02*	Parashat Hayyei Sarah
11/02*	Shabbat Mevarekhim
11/05*	Rosh Chodesh Kislev (Beginning of the month of Kislev)

11/09*	Parashat Toledot
11/16*	Parashat Va-Yeze
11/23*	Parashat Va-Yishlah
11/29*	Erev Hanukkah
11/29*	Light 1st candle
11/30*	Hanukkah (First day)
11/30*	Light 2nd candle

12/01*	Hanukkah (Second day)

12/02*	Hanukkah (Third day)

12/03*	Hanukkah (Fourth day)

12/04*	Hanukkah (Fifth day)
12/04*	Light 6th candle
12/05*	Hanukkah (Sixth day)
12/05*	Light 7th candle
12/05*	Rosh Chodesh Tevet (Beginning of the month of Tevet)
12/06*	Hanukkah (Seventh day)
12/06*	Light 8th candle

#endif /* !_calendar_judaic_ */
